name: Anginares Tarama
recipeInstructions:
1. To clean the artichokes, cut off the stems flush with the base and remove any tough, unsightly outer leaves. Cut off the top one-third of the flower bud itself.
2. Force the leaves open slightly and rinse under cold water with a squeeze of lemon or vinegar to prevent discoloring.
3. Place the artichokes upside-down in a pot large enough to hold them all in one layer. Pour in boiling water to come one-fourth of the way up the sides.
4. Salt and simmer for 15-40 minutes, depending on age and size, until the heart is just tender when pricked with a fork. Drain and refresh under cold water; drain again and chill.
5. Beat the tarama or smoked cod's roe with grated onion in a food processor. If using by hand, be patient and persistent to achieve a light texture.
6. Finely grate the zest of one lemon and beat it in, adding 2 teaspoons boiling water.
7. Squeeze the juice of both lemons. Alternately dribble in lemon juice and olive oil, beating each addition before adding the next.
8. Taste and season with pepper. Remove the inner leaves and hairy choke from the artichokes.
9. Spoon tarama into the cup of leaves.
